reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. `Subgroup.closure` in Mathlib is defined as `sInf { K | k ⊆ K }`, which is exactly "the smallest subgroup containing the set" — the standard formal rendering of "generated by". The coercion `(H : Set G)` yields the underlying set, so `(H : Set G) \ {1}` is exactly the set $H - \{1\}$. The universal quantifier over arbitrary groups G and arbitrary subgroups H matches the informal phrasing. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the trivial-subgroup edge case (where `H \ {1} = ∅` and `closure ∅ = ⊥ = {1}`) is handled correctly, so the statement is logically equivalent to the informal one.
